items.text: What is the main quality of the chest discomfort?
Does the discomfort radiate from the chest?
choices: Radiates to the left or either arm
Radiates to the jaw, neck, or shoulder

items.text: How did the discomfort begin?
choices: No clear onset or not currently symptomatic

items.text: Is the discomfort brought on by exertion?
choices: Occurs with exertion and improves with rest
Occurs at rest or with minimal exertion

items.text: Is the discomfort worse with a deep breath or cough?
choices: Not worse with breathing or cough
Worse with deep breathing or cough

items.text: Does body position change the discomfort?
choices: Worse lying flat and improved leaning forward

items.text: Can movement or pressure on the chest reproduce the discomfort?
choices: Not reproduced by movement or palpation

items.text: Is shortness of breath present with the chest discomfort?
choices: Shortness of breath only with exertion

items.text: Is sweating present with the chest discomfort?
Is nausea or vomiting present with the chest discomfort?
Has the patient had syncope or near-syncope with this episode?
Does the patient have known coronary artery disease?
choices: Known coronary disease without prior revascularization
Prior myocardial infarction, PCI, or CABG

items.text: Which atherosclerotic cardiovascular risk factors are present?
choices: First-degree family history of premature coronary disease

items.text: Which aortic-dissection risk factors are present?
Which venous-thromboembolism risk factors are present?
choices: Prior deep-vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism

items.text: Is the discomfort related to meals or acid suppression?
choices: No relationship to meals or antacids
Burning discomfort after meals or when lying down

items.text: Was there a recent viral illness before the chest discomfort began?
Was a stimulant used within 24 hours before symptom onset?
choices: Amphetamine or other stimulant use within 24 hours

items.text: Measure blood pressure in both arms.
choices: Hypertension without a meaningful inter-arm difference
Inter-arm systolic difference greater than 20 mmHg

items.text: Assess peripheral pulse symmetry.
choices: Pulse diminished or absent in one limb

items.text: Auscultate for a new diastolic murmur.
Auscultate for a pericardial friction rub.
Assess jugular venous pressure.
Auscultate the lungs for asymmetric breath sounds.
choices: Unilateral reduction or absence of breath sounds

items.text: Auscultate the lungs for focal crackles.
Palpate the chest wall for tenderness that reproduces the discomfort.
Inspect and palpate the legs for unilateral swelling or tenderness.
Inspect the painful chest-wall area for a dermatomal vesicular rash.
Assess mental status.
choices: New confusion or altered mental status

items.text: Assess skin perfusion.
